page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-80xCPU-512GB is a KVM-based virtual server utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 9354 processor running at 2.0 GHz, providing 80 physical cores and 80 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation. The system features 512.0 GB of memory, which equates to 6.4 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this configuration, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and VAES.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and passmark CPU score both rank in the top 10% of tested instances. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, with large-block read and write speeds placing in the top 10%, although the passmark memory score falls into the middle 50% average. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, and ray tracing benchmarks all achieve top-tier status. However, single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression, show average performance, and text generation using a medium LLM model is weak, falling into the bottom 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a high concentration of compute cores and memory, making it a viable option for highly parallelized workloads. The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff in environments requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance, as resources are shared with other tenants. Given the lack of local storage, external storage solutions must be integrated. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, software compilation, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-80xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 9354 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It features 80 physical cores, 80 vCPUs under a shared allocation model, and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 6.4 GB per core. This server does not include local storage or a GPU. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation speeds, and large-block memory bandwidth, all ranking in the top 10%. Conversely, single-threaded tasks and medium-model LLM text generation perform at average to weak levels. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs. This instance is suited for parallelized workloads, database operations, and large-scale software compilation.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-80xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 server with 80 AMD EPYC cores and 512 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for parallelized workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-80xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 server featuring 80 AMD EPYC cores and 512 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core-density virtual server featuring eighty AMD EPYC cores and half a terabyte of system memory for parallel processing.
bullet_points: 80 AMD EPYC 9354 cores with shared CPU allocation
512 GB of system memory providing 6.4 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for database operations and highly parallelized workloads
